evals.description: Flask REST API with obvious missing features: no auth, no pagination on list endpoints that could return thousands of rows, hardcoded config, missing input validation on POST, and an explicit TODO for a search endpoint. The skill should surface these gaps as specific, actionable ideas grounded in the actual files.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-brainstorm-0 && cd /tmp/eval-brainstorm-0 && git init && cat > app.py << 'EOF'
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from models import db, Product

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'postgresql://user:password@localhost/proddb'
app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'hardcoded-secret-do-not-ship'

@app.route('/api/products', methods=['GET'])
def list_products():
    # Returns ALL products — no pagination, could be 50k rows
    products = Product.query.all()
    return jsonify([p.to_dict() for p in products])

@app.route('/api/products/<int:id>', methods=['GET'])
def get_product(id):
    p = Product.query.get_or_404(id)
    return jsonify(p.to_dict())

@app.route('/api/products', methods=['POST'])
def create_product():
    # TODO: add input validation
    data = request.json
    p = Product(name=data['name'], price=data['price'], stock=data.get('stock', 0))
    db.session.add(p)
    db.session.commit()
    return jsonify(p.to_dict()), 201

@app.route('/api/products/<int:id>', methods=['PUT'])
def update_product(id):
    p = Product.query.get_or_404(id)
    data = request.json
    p.name = data.get('name', p.name)
    p.price = data.get('price', p.price)
    p.stock = data.get('stock', p.stock)
    db.session.commit()
    return jsonify(p.to_dict())

@app.route('/api/products/<int:id>', methods=['DELETE'])
def delete_product(id):
    p = Product.query.get_or_404(id)
    db.session.delete(p)
    db.session.commit()
    return '', 204

# TODO: add search endpoint — clients keep asking for /api/products?q=keyword

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
EOF
cat > models.py << 'EOF'
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

db = SQLAlchemy()

class Product(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)
    price = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=False)
    stock = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)

    def to_dict(self):
        return {'id': self.id, 'name': self.name, 'price': self.price, 'stock': self.stock}
EOF
cat > requirements.txt << 'EOF'
flask==3.0.0
flask-sqlalchemy==3.1.1
psycopg2-binary==2.9.9
EOF
cat > README.md << 'EOF'
# Product API

A simple REST API for managing product inventory. Built with Flask and PostgreSQL.

## Endpoints
- GET /api/products — list all products
- POST /api/products — create a product
- GET /api/products/:id — get a product
- PUT /api/products/:id — update a product
- DELETE /api/products/:id — delete a product
EOF
git add . && git commit -m 'initial product API'
evals.expected_output: 5 ranked ideas specific to this Flask project. Must surface: (1) pagination on the list endpoint, (2) the search endpoint from the TODO comment, (3) input validation from the TODO on POST, (4) config/secrets management (hardcoded DB URI and SECRET_KEY). Each idea must reference actual file names (app.py, models.py) rather than generic advice. Each idea must have an effort estimate and a specific 'Where to start' step.

assertions.text: The report contains exactly 5 numbered ideas
The TODO comment about adding a search endpoint (clients asking for /api/products?q=keyword) is identified as one of the ideas
The missing pagination on GET /api/products (currently returns all rows with no limit) is identified as a problem
At least 3 of the 5 ideas reference a specific file from the project (app.py or models.py) in their 'Where to start' section
Every idea includes an effort estimate (hours, days, or weeks)

At least one idea is a new user-facing feature (not a technical fix) — something like product categories, low-stock alerts, bulk pricing, discount codes, or a similar capability that adds business value to a product inventory API

evals.prompt: What should I work on next?

The skill must read the source to find these signals rather than producing generic CLI advice.

evals.prompt: Give me ideas for improving this project
evals.description: React task manager app where the git history shows a half-finished 'recurring tasks' feature (branch merged with 'WIP' in commit message, RecurringTask component exists but is never rendered, and the recurrence field exists in the data model but is always null). The skill should notice the abandoned feature and other visible gaps (no due-date sorting, no bulk actions, no keyboard shortcuts). Tests whether the skill reads git history and actual component usage, not just README.
evals.expected_output: 5 ranked ideas specific to Taskmaster. Must surface: (1) complete the abandoned recurring tasks feature (RecurringTask.jsx exists but is commented out in App.jsx, recurrence field is always null), (2) sort tasks by due date (currently insertion order only). Ideas must reference actual component filenames. Effort estimates required for all 5.

assertions.text: The report contains exactly 5 numbered ideas
The half-finished recurring tasks feature (RecurringTask.jsx exists and is commented out in App.jsx, recurrence field is always null) is identified as an idea worth completing
The lack of due-date sorting (tasks shown in insertion order with no sort controls in TaskList.jsx) is identified as a gap
At least 3 of the 5 ideas reference a specific component file (App.jsx, TaskList.jsx, TaskItem.jsx, AddTask.jsx, or RecurringTask.jsx) in their 'Where to start' section
Every idea includes an effort estimate (hours, days, or weeks)

At least one idea is a new user-facing feature (not a technical fix) — something like task sharing/collaboration, reminder notifications, subtasks, project/label grouping, or a similar capability that makes the app more useful as a task manager

evals.prompt: What should I build next for this project?
evals.description: Spring Boot bookstore REST API with no TODOs, FIXMEs, or explicit hints. Issues are only visible from reading the code: list endpoints call findAll() with no pagination, getBook() calls Optional.get() with no @ControllerAdvice so a missing book throws NoSuchElementException (500 instead of 404), getBooksByAuthor() does findAll().stream().filter() instead of a derived query (full table scan), and no @Valid annotations anywhere. The skill must infer these from the code itself.
evals.expected_output: 5 ranked ideas inferred purely from reading the code — no TODO/FIXME hints present. Must identify: (1) pagination missing on list endpoints (findAll() with no Pageable), (2) missing error handling — Optional.get() in BookService.getBook() and AuthorController.getAuthor() will throw NoSuchElementException returning 500 instead of 404, (3) inefficient full-table-scan in BookService.getBooksByAuthor() doing findAll().stream().filter() instead of a JPA derived query. Ideas must reference actual class names.

assertions.text: The report contains exactly 5 numbered ideas
The missing pagination on list endpoints is identified — specifically that getAllBooks() or listBooks() returns all rows with no Pageable/page parameter
The missing error handling is identified — Optional.get() in BookService or AuthorController will throw NoSuchElementException (500) instead of returning a 404 when a resource is not found
At least 3 of the 5 ideas reference a specific class from the project (BookController, BookService, AuthorController, BookRepository, or AuthorRepository) in their 'Where to start' section
Every idea includes an effort estimate (hours, days, or weeks)

At least one idea is a new user-facing feature (not a technical fix) — something like book search/filtering by title or genre, a wishlist or reading list, stock/inventory alerts, order management, book recommendations, or a similar capability that adds business value to a bookstore API

evals.prompt: Give me 5 ideas for this project
evals.description: Go URL shortener with no hints, TODOs, or FIXMEs. Issues are only visible from reading the code: in-memory store (all links lost on restart), math/rand slug generation (not crypto/rand), no click tracking at all, no slug collision check, no custom slug support. Tests whether the skill's imagination lens produces genuinely new business features (click analytics, custom slugs, link expiry, QR codes) rather than just fixing visible code issues.
evals.expected_output: 5 ranked ideas. Must identify: (1) the in-memory store (links lost on restart), (2) the complete absence of click tracking. Must include at least one genuinely new business feature not hinted at in the code — custom slugs, click analytics, link expiry, QR code generation, or similar. Ideas must reference main.go or specific function names.

assertions.text: The report contains exactly 5 numbered ideas
The in-memory store (all links stored in a plain Go map, lost on every restart with no persistence) is identified as a problem
The complete absence of click tracking or analytics is identified as a gap — the redirectHandler does nothing but redirect, with no counting or logging of clicks
At least 3 of the 5 ideas reference main.go or a specific function from the project (generateSlug, shortenHandler, redirectHandler) in their 'Where to start' section
Every idea includes an effort estimate (hours, days, or weeks)

At least one idea is a genuinely new user-facing capability not hinted at anywhere in the code — something like click analytics/dashboard, custom branded slugs, link expiry dates, QR code generation, bulk link management, or a similar capability that makes the service more valuable
